title: artemisia campestris subsp. borealis
intro: Artemisia campestris subsp. borealis, also known as northern wormwood, is a subspecies of the Artemisia campestris plant, a perennial herb native to northern regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve flatulence, and act as a mild diuretic. Northern wormwood has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Northern wormwood can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies for its digestive and immune-boosting benefits.
